what_is.title: What is PDFOrca?
what_is.subtitle: A free, privacy-first PDF toolkit built by an independent developer in India.
what_is.p1: PDFOrca is a free online platform that lets you work with PDF files without installing software, creating an account, or handing your documents to a company you know nothing about. Upload a file, pick a tool, and download the result — that is the entire workflow.
what_is.p2: The platform currently offers 21 tools covering the tasks people need most: merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting a document into separate files, compressing oversized reports, converting between PDF and formats like Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and JPG, adding watermarks or page numbers, protecting files with a password, and running OCR on scanned pages to make them searchable.
what_is.p3: PDFOrca also includes AI-powered analysis. Upload a research paper, contract, or business report and the AI will generate a concise summary, extract the key points, and suggest a descriptive title — useful when you need to decide whether a long document is worth reading in full.
what_is.how_step1_text: Drag and drop your PDF (or Word, Excel, JPG) into the tool. No signup required.
what_is.how_step2_text: Your file is processed over an encrypted connection and automatically deleted within one hour.
what_is.how_step3_text: Download the result instantly. No watermarks, no quality loss, no hidden charges.
what_is.diff1: Every core tool is free with no daily limits or signup walls — not a trial, not a teaser.
what_is.diff2: Files are processed in temporary server memory and deleted within one hour. We never store your documents.
what_is.diff3: The interface is available in English and Hindi, with support for Indian document types like Aadhaar and government forms.
what_is.diff4: Built and maintained by one independent developer. No venture funding, no ad-tech partners, no data selling.

merge_pdf.title: Merge PDF
merge_pdf.description: Combine multiple PDF files into a single document. Upload your files, arrange them in order, and download the merged result.
merge_pdf.seo_h1: Merge PDF Files Online for Free

seo_faq.question: Can I merge password-protected PDFs?
seo_faq.answer: You'll need to unlock each PDF first using our Unlock PDF tool, then merge the unprotected files. The merged result can be re-protected afterwards.

seo_faq.question: Can I reorder pages before merging?

merge_pdf.seo_content:
Merging PDFs is one of those small tasks that turns up constantly at work and at home. You might have a scanned contract split across three files, a set of monthly invoices your accountant wants as one document, or the separate chapters of a report that need to become a single deliverable. PDFOrca's merge tool combines any number of PDFs into one ordered file, right in your browser, without installing software or creating an account.

The workflow is deliberately simple. Drop in the files you want to join, drag the thumbnails until the order looks right, and download the result. Because you control the sequence before merging, the finished document reads exactly the way you intend — cover page first, appendices last, and nothing out of place.

When merging PDFs actually helps

  • Job and grant applications that ask for a single attachment containing your CV, cover letter and certificates.
  • Bookkeeping, where dozens of receipts are far easier to file and email as one PDF than as loose scans.
  • Study notes and e-books assembled from individually downloaded sections.
  • Legal and property paperwork that must be submitted as one continuous document.

Quality and privacy

Merging never re-encodes your pages, so text stays selectable, fonts stay embedded, and images keep their original resolution — the output is a faithful copy of your inputs, simply joined together.

There's no watermark on the finished file and no page limit hidden behind a paywall for everyday use. If you regularly prepare bundles — think onboarding packs or client reports — you can merge, then run the result through our Compress PDF tool to keep the file small enough to email, or use Organize PDF to fine-tune the page order afterwards.

Tips for a clean merge

Name your files with a leading number (01, 02, 03…) before uploading and they'll line up in the right order automatically. If a document is rotated the wrong way, fix it first with Rotate PDF — the merge tool preserves each page's orientation exactly as supplied. And if you only need a few pages from a larger file, pull them out with Extract Pages before combining, so the final PDF contains only what matters.

compress_pdf.title: Compress PDF
compress_pdf.description: Reduce PDF file size while maintaining quality. Choose compression level based on your needs.
compress_pdf.seo_h1: Compress PDF Files Online for Free

seo_faq.question: Is PDF compression safe?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, compression only optimizes images and removes unnecessary metadata. Your document content remains completely intact.

seo_faq.question: How much can I reduce my PDF size?
seo_faq.answer: Depending on the content, you can reduce file size by 20–90%. PDFs with large images benefit the most from compression.

seo_faq.question: Will compression affect the quality of my PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Low and medium compression preserve excellent visual quality. High compression reduces size the most but may slightly affect image sharpness.

seo_faq.question: Is there a file size limit?
seo_faq.answer: You can compress PDFs up to 100 MB. There is no daily limit on how many files you can compress.

compress_pdf.seo_content:
A PDF that won't attach to an email or takes forever to upload is almost always carrying oversized images. Photos embedded at full camera resolution, high-DPI scans and un-optimised graphics can push a document into the tens of megabytes even when it's only a few pages long. PDFOrca's compressor shrinks that file back down to a practical size while keeping it perfectly readable.

Compression works by re-sampling and re-encoding the images inside your PDF and stripping redundant data the viewer never needed. Plain text and vector elements — headings, tables, line art — stay crisp because they aren't image-based to begin with. The result is a lighter file that still looks right on screen and in print.

Why file size matters

  • Email limits: most inboxes reject attachments over 20–25 MB, so a bloated scan simply bounces.
  • Web forms and portals: government and banking uploads frequently cap files at 2 MB or 5 MB.
  • Storage and sharing: smaller PDFs sync faster and use less space across every device.

Balancing size against quality

There's always a trade-off between how small a file becomes and how sharp its images stay. For documents that are mostly text with the odd logo, aggressive compression is invisible. For photo-heavy brochures or portfolios, a lighter setting preserves detail. If a scanned document looks fuzzy after compression, the original scan resolution was probably the real culprit — try re-scanning at 200–300 DPI rather than 600.

Getting the smallest usable file

If a document is still too large after compressing, the images inside are likely enormous. Converting a photo-only PDF back through PDF to JPG and then JPG to PDF at a sensible resolution can help. When you're bundling several files, compress each one before you merge them — it's far more effective than compressing one giant merged file at the end. Scanned paperwork specifically benefits from our OCR step too, which makes the text searchable without inflating the file.

split_pdf.title: Split PDF
split_pdf.description: Split a PDF into multiple files or extract specific pages.
split_pdf.seo_h1: Split PDF Files Online for Free

seo_faq.question: Can I split a PDF into individual pages?
seo_faq.answer: Yes! Our tool lets you split a PDF into individual pages, by page range, or every N pages — whatever you need.

seo_faq.question: How do I extract specific pages from a PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Use 'Extract Pages' mode and enter the page numbers you want, separated by commas (e.g. 1, 3, 5-7). The tool creates a new PDF with only those pages.

seo_faq.question: Will splitting change the PDF quality?
seo_faq.answer: No. Splitting only separates pages — the content, images, and formatting of each page remain exactly as in the original.

seo_faq.question: Is the split PDF downloaded as a ZIP?
seo_faq.answer: When splitting into multiple files, the result is packaged as a ZIP archive for easy download. Single-file extractions download directly.

split_pdf.seo_content:
Sometimes you don't need the whole document — you need page 4, or chapter two, or every invoice as its own file. Splitting a PDF lets you break one large document into smaller pieces or pull out just the pages you want, without printing and re-scanning anything. PDFOrca's split tool does this in the browser and hands back exactly the pages you asked for.

You can split by a fixed range (say, pages 1–10 and 11–20), extract a handful of specific pages, or break a bundle into individual single-page files. This is ideal when a scanner has saved a hundred documents as one endless PDF, or when a shared report contains a section that only one colleague needs.

Common reasons to split a PDF

  • Sharing selectively — send someone only the pages relevant to them instead of the entire file.
  • Separating batch scans — turn one long scan into one file per document.
  • Trimming size — a smaller subset of pages is quicker to email and upload.
  • Reworking a document — split, edit the pieces, then reassemble.

Accurate, lossless splitting

Splitting copies the selected pages exactly as they are — no re-compression, no change to text or image quality. Bookmarks and page content stay intact within the range you keep.

Split, then do more

Splitting pairs naturally with the rest of the toolkit. Break a document apart, reorder the pieces with Organize PDF, and merge them back into a cleaner sequence. If you only ever want a couple of pages, Extract Pages is a faster route to the same result. And when the pages you've pulled out are still heavy with scanned images, run them through Compress PDF so the new, smaller files are genuinely lightweight.

pdf_to_word.description: Convert PDF files to editable Word documents (.docx) with high accuracy.

pdf_to_word.seo_h2: How to Convert PDF to Word

seo_faq.question: Will the formatting be preserved?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, our converter preserves the original layout, fonts, images, and table structures in the resulting Word document.

seo_faq.question: Can I convert a scanned PDF to Word?
seo_faq.answer: Yes! Our tool uses OCR (Optical Character Recognition) to extract text from scanned PDFs and create editable Word documents.

seo_faq.question: Is there a file size limit?
seo_faq.answer: You can convert PDFs up to 100 MB. There is no daily limit on the number of conversions.

seo_faq.question: What Word format will I receive?
seo_faq.answer: You'll receive a .docx file compatible with Microsoft Word, Google Docs, LibreOffice, and other word processors.

pdf_to_word.seo_content:
PDFs are built for viewing, not editing — which is frustrating the moment you need to change a sentence, update a figure or reuse a paragraph. Converting a PDF to an editable Word document (.docx) gives you back a file you can open in Microsoft Word, Google Docs or LibreOffice and edit like any other document. PDFOrca reconstructs the text, headings, tables and layout so you're not retyping anything.

The converter reads the structure of your PDF — paragraphs, lists, columns and tables — and rebuilds it as native Word content rather than a flat image. That means you can place your cursor anywhere and start typing, restyle headings, or copy a table straight into another document.

Where a Word version saves the day

  • Updating a contract or template you were only sent as a PDF.
  • Reusing content — lifting paragraphs or tables into a new report.
  • Fixing a typo in a finished document without recreating it from scratch.
  • Translating or reformatting text that's locked inside a PDF.

What to expect from the conversion

A PDF that was originally created from Word or a similar program converts almost perfectly, because the underlying text is real, selectable text. Scanned PDFs are different — they're pictures of pages, so they first need optical character recognition to become editable words. For those, run the file through our OCR tool first, then convert. Very complex layouts with heavy graphic design may need light touch-ups after conversion, but the bulk of the work is done for you.

Related conversions

Going the other way — turning a finished Word file back into a polished, uneditable PDF — is handled by Word to PDF. If your PDF is really a spreadsheet in disguise, PDF to Excel will recover the numbers into cells, and slide decks come across cleanly with PDF to PowerPoint.

word_to_pdf.seo_h1: Convert Word to PDF Online for Free
word_to_pdf.seo_h2: How to Convert Word to PDF

seo_faq.question: Will the Word formatting be preserved?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Fonts, images, tables, headers, footers, bullet lists, and page breaks are all carried across exactly as they appear in your original Word document. The converter renders the file the same way Word's print engine does, so the PDF is a pixel-accurate snapshot of your layout.

seo_faq.question: Can I convert .doc and .docx files?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, both the older .doc format (Word 97–2003) and the modern .docx format (Word 2007 and later) are fully supported. Simply upload whichever version you have and the converter handles the rest — no need to re-save in a different format first.

seo_faq.question: Does Word to PDF preserve headers and footers?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Headers, footers, page numbers, and footnotes from your Word document are rendered in the PDF exactly as they appear in print preview.

seo_faq.question: Will the PDF be editable?
seo_faq.answer: By default, the output PDF preserves your document layout in a fixed, non-editable format — ideal for sharing, printing, and archiving. If you later need to make changes, you can convert it back to Word using our PDF to Word tool.

seo_faq.question: What happens to hyperlinks in my Word document?
seo_faq.answer: Clickable hyperlinks in your Word file — including URLs, email links, and internal cross-references — are preserved as active links in the resulting PDF. Recipients can click them just as they would in the original document.

word_to_pdf.why_convert_universal: Universal: PDFs render the same on every device and OS.
word_to_pdf.why_convert_professional: Professional: Print-ready output ideal for business sharing.
word_to_pdf.seo_content:
When you send a Word document to someone else, there's no guarantee it will look the way you designed it. A missing font, a different version of Word, or Google Docs opening the file can shift your margins, break your page layout and move images around. Converting to PDF locks the document down: everyone sees the exact same pages, fonts and spacing, on any device.

PDFOrca turns .doc and .docx files into clean, print-ready PDFs while preserving your formatting — headings, bullet lists, tables, headers and footers, embedded images and page breaks all stay put. The finished PDF is the professional, tamper-resistant version of your document that's expected for anything official.

When you should convert to PDF

  • Sending a CV or cover letter — recruiters expect a PDF that prints identically everywhere.
  • Invoices and quotes that shouldn't be accidentally edited by the recipient.
  • Official submissions — many portals only accept PDF uploads.
  • Archiving — a PDF stays readable for years without depending on a specific Word version.

Faithful layout, every time

The conversion renders your document exactly as Word would print it, so what you see in the preview is what your reader gets. Fonts are embedded into the PDF, which is why the text never reflows or substitutes on the recipient's machine. Complex elements like multi-column layouts, footnotes and tables of contents are carried across intact.

Practical tips for a clean conversion

  • Embed your fonts — if your document uses a decorative or uncommon typeface, embedding it in Word (File → Options → Save → Embed fonts) guarantees it appears correctly in the PDF.
  • Check your page breaks — manually inserted page breaks convert more predictably than relying on automatic breaks, especially in long documents with mixed layouts.
  • Use high-resolution images — photos and diagrams placed at 150 DPI or above will stay sharp in the PDF. Low-resolution images may look acceptable on screen but blur when printed.
  • Review headers and footers — make sure page numbers, dates, and running titles display correctly in Print Preview before uploading, since the converter renders them exactly as Word would print them.

After you convert

If the resulting PDF is large because of embedded photos, Compress PDF will trim it for emailing. Need to combine your new PDF with other documents — a signed form, an appendix — use Merge PDF. And should you ever need to edit the content again, PDF to Word converts it back into an editable document.

pdf_to_jpg.title: PDF to JPG
pdf_to_jpg.description: Convert PDF pages to high-quality JPG images. Extract all pages or specific ones.
pdf_to_jpg.seo_h1: Convert PDF to JPG Online for Free
pdf_to_jpg.seo_h2: How to Convert PDF to JPG

seo_faq.question: Can I convert all pages at once?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. When you select 'All Pages', every page of your PDF is converted to a separate JPG image and delivered together in a single ZIP archive. You can then extract the ZIP and use each image individually — no need to convert pages one at a time.

seo_faq.question: What quality will the images be?
seo_faq.answer: You can choose the output quality using the quality slider. Higher quality (90–100%) produces sharper, print-ready images at larger file sizes, while lower quality (60–75%) creates smaller files ideal for web use or email attachments. The DPI setting also lets you control resolution.

seo_faq.question: Can I extract just one page?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Switch to 'Specific Page' mode and enter the page number you want. Only that page is converted and downloaded as a standalone JPG, which is useful when you need a single chart, certificate, or diagram from a longer document.

seo_faq.question: What DPI are the output images?
seo_faq.answer: Images are exported at 150 DPI by default, which balances clarity with file size. This is sharp enough for on-screen reading and most print uses.

seo_faq.question: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
seo_faq.answer: You can upload PDFs up to 100 MB for free. For very large files or documents with many pages, the tool automatically adjusts the DPI setting if needed to ensure the conversion completes without timing out.

pdf_to_jpg.seo_content:
Sometimes a PDF page is more useful as an image. Maybe you want to post a page on social media, drop a diagram into a slide, use a certificate as a thumbnail, or preview a document where PDFs aren't supported. Converting PDF to JPG turns each page into a standalone image you can view, share and embed anywhere images are accepted.

PDFOrca renders every page of your PDF as a high-quality JPG. Whether it's a single certificate or a multi-page report, you get one crisp image per page, ready to download and reuse.

When an image beats a PDF

  • Social media and messaging apps that preview images but not PDF attachments.
  • Presentations and documents where you want to drop a page in as a picture.
  • Website content — showing a page inline without an embedded PDF viewer.
  • Quick previews of a document's contents in a photo gallery.

Clarity you can rely on

Pages are rendered at a resolution that keeps text sharp and fine detail intact, so a converted invoice or diagram stays readable rather than turning blurry. Vector graphics and text are rasterised cleanly at export time, which usually looks better than screenshotting a page yourself. If you need the images smaller for the web, compress them after export.

Practical tips for better results

  • Choose the right DPI — 150 DPI is usually enough for on-screen viewing and social media. Use 300 DPI when you plan to print the image or need fine text to remain razor-sharp.
  • Use quality 80–90% for the best balance — this keeps file sizes manageable while preserving detail. Going above 95% rarely improves visible quality but can double the file size.
  • Extract specific pages first — if you only need a few pages from a long PDF, use Extract Pages to pull them out before converting. This avoids downloading a large ZIP full of images you don't need.
  • Consider PNG for text-heavy pages — JPG is ideal for photographs and coloured graphics, but pages that are mostly text and line art may look slightly crisper as PNG images at comparable file sizes.

Related tools

Going the other way — turning a batch of images back into a single document — is what JPG to PDF is for. If you only need images from certain pages, pull those pages out first with Extract Pages, then convert. And when you'd rather keep everything as a PDF but make it smaller, Compress PDF is the better choice than converting to images.

seo_faq.answer: We support JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP, BMP, and TIFF image formats.

seo_faq.question: Can I choose the page size and orientation?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, you can choose between portrait and landscape orientation and page sizes like A4, Letter, and Legal.

seo_faq.question: Can I combine multiple images into one PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. You can upload several images at once and they'll be combined into a single PDF, one image per page, in the order you arrange them.

Photos and scans are convenient to capture but awkward to submit. A folder full of JPGs is easy to lose track of, hard to print in order, and often rejected by forms that only accept PDFs. Converting your images to a single PDF turns a loose set of pictures into one tidy, shareable document — perfect for photographed paperwork, receipts and ID pages.

PDFOrca lets you upload multiple JPG, JPEG or PNG images, arrange them in the order you want, and combine them into one PDF where each image becomes a page. It's the quickest way to make a phone photo of a document behave like a proper scan.

Everyday uses

  • Turning phone photos of documents into a single PDF a portal will accept.
  • Building a photo album or portfolio that opens as one file.
  • Combining front and back of an ID card or cheque into one page-ordered document.
  • Submitting receipts for an expense claim as one attachment.

Sharp images, sensible sizes

Each image is placed on its own page at a quality that stays true to the original, so text in a photographed document remains legible. If your source photos are very large, the resulting PDF can be too — running it through Compress PDF afterwards keeps it email-friendly without visibly hurting quality. Good lighting and a steady, straight-on photo make the biggest difference to how professional the finished PDF looks.

Do more with your images

Photographed a document that you need as searchable text? Convert to PDF here, then run OCR so the words become selectable. To combine your new image-PDF with other files, use Merge PDF, and if the pages come out sideways, straighten them with Rotate PDF. Going the other direction — pulling images back out of a PDF — is handled by PDF to JPG.

protect_pdf.title: Protect PDF
protect_pdf.description: Add password protection and encryption to your PDF files.
protect_pdf.seo_h1: Protect PDF with Password Online for Free
protect_pdf.seo_h2: How to Password Protect a PDF

seo_faq.question: What encryption standard is used?
seo_faq.answer: We use AES-256 encryption — the same military-grade standard used by banks and governments — to protect your PDF.

seo_faq.question: Can I control what the recipient can do with the PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, you can restrict printing, copying text, and modifying the document independently from the open password.

seo_faq.question: What happens if I forget the password?
seo_faq.answer: There is no way to recover a lost password for an encrypted PDF. Please save your password somewhere safe before applying protection.

seo_faq.question: Is the protection permanent?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, the password protection is embedded in the PDF itself, so it remains active regardless of how the file is shared or stored.

protect_pdf.seo_content:
Some documents simply shouldn't be readable by anyone who happens to get hold of the file — bank statements, medical records, contracts, payslips and ID scans among them. Password-protecting a PDF encrypts its contents so that only someone with the password can open it. If the file is forwarded, leaked or intercepted, it stays unreadable without the key.

PDFOrca adds strong AES encryption to your PDF and locks it behind a password you choose. From that point on, opening the document requires the password, giving you real control over who can see what's inside.

When you should protect a PDF

  • Emailing sensitive files — a locked attachment is safe even if it lands in the wrong inbox.
  • Sharing financial or legal documents that must stay confidential.
  • Storing personal records where a device might be lost or shared.
  • Distributing paid content you want to keep behind a password.

Choosing a password that actually protects you

Encryption is only as strong as the password behind it. Use something long and unpredictable rather than a name or date, and share it through a different channel than the file itself — text the password if you emailed the document, for instance. Crucially, there's no back door: if you forget the password, the file genuinely can't be opened, so store it somewhere safe. A password protects who can open the file; if you also want to visibly mark it, add a label with Add Watermark.

Related tools

If you're the owner of a file whose password has become inconvenient, Unlock PDF removes it. Bundle documents together with Merge PDF before protecting the whole set, and shrink a large file for sharing with Compress PDF — then apply the password last.

organize_pdf.title: Organize PDF
organize_pdf.description: Reorder, rotate, or delete pages in your PDF document.
organize_pdf.seo_h1: Organize PDF Pages Online for Free

seo_faq.question: Can I organize multiple PDFs at once?
seo_faq.answer: Yes! You can upload multiple PDF files and organize their pages together as if they were a single document.

seo_faq.question: How do I reorder pages?
seo_faq.answer: Simply drag and drop page thumbnails to rearrange them. You can also use our sorting options to automatically organize pages ascending or descending.

seo_faq.question: Can I delete pages from my PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Absolutely. Hover over any page and click the trash icon to delete it. Use 'Reset to Original' to restore all deleted pages.

seo_faq.question: Is there a limit to the number of pages I can organize?
seo_faq.answer: PDFOrca supports PDFs with up to 500 pages. For larger documents, consider splitting them into smaller sections first using our Split PDF tool.

Documents don't always come together in the right order. A scan might have pages back to front, a merged file might need its sections rearranged, or you might want to drop a couple of blank or irrelevant pages entirely. Organizing a PDF lets you reorder, rearrange and remove pages until the document flows exactly the way it should — all from a visual view of your pages.

PDFOrca shows each page as a thumbnail so you can drag them into a new sequence and delete the ones you don't need. It's the tidiest way to fix a document's structure without rebuilding it from scratch.

What you can sort out

  • Reordering pages that were scanned or merged out of sequence.
  • Removing blanks, duplicates or pages that don't belong.
  • Moving an appendix or cover page to where it should sit.
  • Cleaning up a bundle before you send or archive it.

A clear view of your document

Working visually with thumbnails makes reorganising far less error-prone than juggling page numbers in your head — you can see at a glance where each page will end up. Rearranging and removing pages doesn't re-compress the ones you keep, so quality is untouched. It's the natural finishing step after merging several files, when the combined order needs a little polish.

Related tools

Organizing works hand in hand with the rest of the toolkit. Use Merge PDF to combine files first, then organize the order here. If you'd rather pull specific pages into a new document, use Extract Pages, or break a file into parts with Split PDF. Pages facing the wrong way can be straightened with Rotate PDF.

add_watermark.title: Add Watermark
add_watermark.description: Add text or image watermarks to your PDF pages.
add_watermark.seo_h1: Add Watermark to PDF Online for Free
add_watermark.seo_h2: How to Add a Watermark to PDF

seo_faq.question: Can I customize the watermark appearance?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. You have full control over the text content, font size (from 12pt to 60pt), colour, opacity, rotation angle, and position on the page. This lets you create anything from a faint diagonal 'DRAFT' label to a bold centred company name.

seo_faq.question: Can I watermark only specific pages?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. You can apply the watermark to all pages, only the first page, or a custom range (for example, pages 1–5 and 10–12). This is useful when the cover page or appendix should remain unmarked while the main content is labelled.

seo_faq.question: Will the watermark cover my content?
seo_faq.answer: You can control the opacity to make the watermark as subtle or prominent as you like. A typical setting of 30–50% opacity is clearly visible but does not obscure the text and images beneath it. You can also adjust the position to avoid key content areas.

seo_faq.question: Can I watermark only specific pages?
seo_faq.answer: Currently the watermark is applied to every page. To watermark a subset, split the PDF first, watermark the pages you need, then merge the parts back together.

seo_faq.question: Can I remove a watermark later?
seo_faq.answer: A watermark added by this tool becomes part of the PDF's visual layer, so it cannot be removed with a single click. Always keep a copy of your original unwatermarked PDF. If you need to share a clean version later, you can go back to the original and re-watermark with different settings.

add_watermark.seo_content:
A watermark is a simple way to mark a document as yours, flag its status, or discourage unauthorised reuse. Whether it's a faint "CONFIDENTIAL" across a report, a "DRAFT" stamp on a work in progress, or your name and website over a portfolio, a watermark communicates ownership and intent at a glance. PDFOrca lets you add text watermarks across every page of a PDF in a few clicks.

You control the wording, and the watermark is applied consistently to the whole document so no page is left unmarked. It's the quickest way to brand or label a batch of pages without editing each one.

Common uses

  • Marking status — DRAFT, COPY, SAMPLE or CONFIDENTIAL across sensitive files.
  • Branding — adding your company name or website to shared documents.
  • Protecting creative work — labelling portfolios, proofs and previews before delivery.
  • Discouraging misuse — making it clear a document isn't the final, licensed version.

Getting a professional result

A good watermark is legible without overwhelming the content beneath it — a semi-transparent, diagonal label usually strikes the right balance. Keep the text short so it reads clearly at a glance, and remember that a watermark deters casual reuse but isn't a security control on its own. If you need to genuinely restrict who can open a file, combine a watermark with a password using Protect PDF.

Practical tips for effective watermarks

  • Keep the text short — one or two words like "DRAFT" or "CONFIDENTIAL" are more effective than a full sentence. Short labels remain readable even when rotated diagonally across a page.
  • Use 30–50% opacity — this range makes the watermark clearly visible without making the underlying content difficult to read. Go higher (60–70%) only for proofs or samples where you deliberately want to discourage reuse.
  • Save the original — always keep an unwatermarked copy of your PDF. Once a watermark is embedded it becomes part of the document's visual layer, so having the clean original lets you re-watermark with different settings later.
  • Combine with password protection — a watermark labels a document but doesn't prevent someone from opening it. For truly confidential files, add a password via Protect PDF after watermarking.

Related tools

Watermarking pairs well with the rest of your workflow: merge documents first so a single watermark covers the whole bundle, then compress the result for sharing. To lock the watermarked file so it can't be opened without a password, finish with Protect PDF.

unlock_pdf.title: Unlock PDF
unlock_pdf.description: Remove password protection from a PDF you have the password to.
unlock_pdf.password_hint: We use this password only to decrypt the file. It is never stored.
unlock_pdf.seo_h2: How to Remove a Password from a PDF

seo_faq.question: Can I unlock any PDF?
seo_faq.answer: This tool is intended for PDFs you own or are authorised to unlock. It removes password restrictions from documents you have the right to access — it isn't a way to bypass security on files that don't belong to you.

seo_faq.question: Do I need the current password?
seo_faq.answer: If a PDF requires a password simply to open it, you'll normally need to know that password. Unlocking is mainly about removing restrictions like blocked printing or copying on files you can already open.

seo_faq.question: What restrictions does unlocking remove?
seo_faq.answer: It removes the password prompt and permission limits such as no-printing or no-copying, giving you an unrestricted copy you can open, print and edit freely.

seo_faq.question: Can I re-protect the file later?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. If you want to secure the document again with a new password, use our Protect PDF tool to add fresh AES encryption.

seo_faq.question: What types of PDF encryption can be removed?
seo_faq.answer: The tool removes standard password-based encryption (both owner and user passwords). If you know the user password, enter it and the restrictions are lifted. DRM or certificate-based protection is not supported.

unlock_pdf.seo_content:
A password-protected PDF is great for security right up until it becomes a nuisance — retyping the password every time you open a file you already own, or being unable to print or copy from a document you have every right to use. Unlocking a PDF removes that password prompt and the associated restrictions, so you can work with the file freely.

PDFOrca's unlock tool removes the password from PDFs you're authorised to access, producing an unrestricted copy you can open, print and edit without repeatedly entering credentials.

When unlocking is the right call

  • Your own files where the password has become an everyday obstacle.
  • Documents you're allowed to use but which block printing or copying.
  • Statements and bills that arrive locked and are awkward to file.
  • Archiving a collection of protected PDFs into a single accessible set.

An important note on permissions

This tool is for documents you own or are permitted to unlock. If a PDF requires a password just to open it, you'll generally need to know that password — unlocking is about removing the restriction, not defeating encryption on files you have no right to access. Please respect the rights of document owners and only remove protection where you're authorised to do so.

Related tools

Once a file is unlocked, you can finally work with it: convert it to Word for editing, merge it with other documents, or compress it for sharing. If you later want to secure the file again with a new password, use Protect PDF.

rotate_pdf.title: Rotate PDF
rotate_pdf.description: Rotate all or selected pages of a PDF by 90, 180, or 270 degrees.
rotate_pdf.range_hint: Use 'all' for every page, or a comma list like 1,3,5-7.
rotate_pdf.seo_h1: Rotate PDF Pages Online for Free

seo_faq.question: Is the rotation saved permanently?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. The new orientation is written into the PDF file itself, so anyone who opens, prints or shares it afterwards sees the pages the right way up — unlike temporarily rotating the view in a reader.

seo_faq.question: Can I rotate just one page?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. You can rotate an individual page or the entire document. This is handy when a single page in an otherwise correct file was scanned sideways.

seo_faq.question: Does rotating reduce quality?
seo_faq.answer: No. Rotation simply reorients the existing pages without re-compressing them, so text and images stay exactly as sharp as the original.

seo_faq.question: Which directions can I rotate?
seo_faq.answer: You can rotate in 90-degree steps — clockwise, anticlockwise or a full 180 degrees to flip an upside-down page.

seo_faq.question: Does rotating affect the text content or links?
seo_faq.answer: No. Rotation changes only the page orientation — all text, images, hyperlinks, and form fields remain intact and functional.

rotate_pdf.seo_content:
Scanned documents have a habit of arriving sideways or upside down, especially when pages were fed through the scanner in different orientations. Reading a rotated PDF means constantly tilting your head or your screen. PDFOrca's rotate tool fixes the orientation permanently, so every page sits the right way up and stays that way when you save, share or print.

You can rotate a single stubborn page or spin the whole document at once, in 90-degree steps, until everything reads correctly. The change is saved into the file itself — not just applied in your viewer — so the corrected orientation travels with the document wherever it goes.

When you'll reach for it

  • Fixing scans where pages came out landscape or flipped.
  • Correcting phone-photo PDFs taken at an angle.
  • Preparing documents for print so they don't emerge sideways.
  • Tidying a merged file where one source document was rotated differently.

Permanent, lossless rotation

Rotating doesn't re-compress or degrade your pages — the content is identical, simply reoriented. That matters for scanned text and photos, which stay exactly as sharp as the original. Unlike temporarily rotating the view in a PDF reader, saving here bakes the correct orientation into the file, so the next person to open it sees it upright too.

Rotate as part of a bigger cleanup

Rotation is often the finishing touch on a document you've assembled from several sources. Straighten the pages here, then merge everything into one file, or reorder pages with Organize PDF. If a rotated scan is also bloated, Compress PDF will trim it, and running OCR afterwards makes the now-upright text searchable.

extract_pages.title: Extract Pages
extract_pages.description: Pull selected pages from a PDF into a new single PDF.
extract_pages.pages_hint: Use a comma-separated list. Ranges like 5-7 are supported.
extract_pages.seo_h1: Extract Pages from PDF Online for Free
extract_pages.seo_h2: How to Extract Pages from a PDF

seo_faq.question: How do I choose which pages to extract?
seo_faq.answer: Enter the specific page numbers or a range (for example 3, 5, 8–10) and the tool saves just those pages as a new PDF, leaving your original unchanged.

seo_faq.question: What's the difference between extracting and splitting?
seo_faq.answer: Extracting pulls selected pages into one new file. Splitting breaks a document into multiple separate files. Use Extract Pages when you want a specific subset, and Split PDF when you want to divide a document into parts.

seo_faq.question: Does the original file get modified?
seo_faq.answer: No. Extraction creates a brand-new PDF containing only your chosen pages. The file you upload is never altered.

seo_faq.question: Will the extracted pages lose any quality?
seo_faq.answer: No. The selected pages are copied exactly as they are, with no re-compression, so text and images stay identical to the source.

seo_faq.question: Can I extract non-consecutive pages?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. You can select any combination of pages — for example pages 1, 4, and 7–12 — and they'll be combined into a single new PDF in the order you specified.

extract_pages.seo_content:
You rarely need an entire document — often it's one or two pages that matter: a single certificate from a bundle, the signature page of a contract, or the two relevant pages of a fifty-page report. Extracting pages lets you pull exactly the pages you want into a new PDF and leave the rest behind, without printing and rescanning.

PDFOrca lets you pick specific page numbers or a range and saves just those pages as a fresh document. The original stays untouched; you simply get a lean new file containing only what you selected.

Handy situations

  • Sending one section of a long report to a colleague who doesn't need the rest.
  • Isolating a form or certificate from a larger scanned bundle.
  • Pulling the pages you'll sign or fill in from a big agreement.
  • Cutting a document down so it's small enough to email or upload.

Exact pages, unchanged quality

Extraction copies the chosen pages precisely as they are — no re-compression and no change to text or image quality. The new PDF is genuinely smaller because it contains fewer pages, which makes it faster to share and easier to fit under upload limits. It's a cleaner approach than deleting pages one by one in a bulky editor.

Related tools

If you'd rather break a document into several parts than pull out a few pages, use Split PDF. To combine extracted pages from different files into one document, follow up with Merge PDF, and to change the running order of the pages you keep, use Organize PDF.

powerpoint_to_pdf.title: PowerPoint to PDF
powerpoint_to_pdf.description: Convert .ppt or .pptx slide decks to PDF, preserving layout and images.
powerpoint_to_pdf.seo_h1: Convert PowerPoint to PDF Online for Free
powerpoint_to_pdf.seo_h2: How to Convert PPT to PDF

seo_faq.question: Does my presentation keep its fonts and layout?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Fonts are embedded into the PDF and each slide becomes a page, so the document looks exactly as designed even on a computer that doesn't have your fonts installed.

seo_faq.question: How are slides arranged in the PDF?
seo_faq.answer: One slide per page, in the same order as your presentation, which makes the PDF ideal for printing as handouts.

seo_faq.question: Will animations be included?
seo_faq.answer: No. A PDF is a static document, so animations and transitions are flattened — each slide appears in its final state. If you need motion, keep the original PowerPoint file for presenting.

seo_faq.question: Can I make the PDF smaller for email?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. If your deck has many high-resolution images, run the converted PDF through our Compress PDF tool to reduce the file size while keeping it readable.

seo_faq.question: Are slide animations preserved in the PDF?
seo_faq.answer: PDF is a static format, so animations and transitions are flattened. Each slide becomes a single page showing the final state of all elements.

powerpoint_to_pdf.seo_content:
Sharing a PowerPoint file has a few pitfalls: the recipient might not have PowerPoint, their fonts may differ, and a stray click can rearrange your carefully built slides. Converting your presentation to PDF solves all three at once — the deck becomes a fixed document that looks identical for everyone and can't be accidentally edited.

PDFOrca converts .ppt and .pptx files into clean PDFs where each slide becomes a page, preserving your layout, fonts, images and colours. It's the format people expect when you're distributing a deck for reading, printing or handing out rather than presenting live.

Why send a deck as PDF

  • Handouts — a PDF prints predictably, one slide per page.
  • Wide compatibility — anyone can open a PDF without presentation software.
  • Protecting your work — viewers can read but not rearrange your slides.
  • Email and uploads — a single PDF is tidier than a bulky .pptx.

Slides that look right on paper

Because fonts are embedded in the PDF, your typography stays exactly as designed even on a machine that doesn't have those fonts installed. Full-bleed backgrounds, charts and high-resolution images are rendered faithfully. If your deck is heavy with photos and the PDF comes out large, run it through Compress PDF to make it easy to email without a visible drop in quality.

Related tools

Need to edit a deck that only exists as a PDF? PDF to PowerPoint converts it back into editable slides. To combine your slides with a cover page or report, use Merge PDF, and to share only selected slides, extract those pages with Extract Pages after converting.

seo_faq.question: Can I skip the first page or title page?
seo_faq.answer: Yes! You can specify which pages should be numbered. Common setups include skipping the cover page, starting numbering from page 2 or 3, or using Roman numerals for front matter.

seo_faq.question: What number formats are available?
seo_faq.answer: We support Arabic numerals (1, 2, 3), Roman numerals (I, II, III, i, ii, iii), alphabetic (A, B, C), and custom formats. You can also add prefixes like 'Page' or suffixes like 'of 50'.

seo_faq.question: Can I add page numbers to multiple PDFs at once?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, you can upload multiple PDF files and apply the same page numbering settings to all of them simultaneously, saving you time on batch processing.

seo_faq.question: Will adding page numbers affect my document's layout?
seo_faq.answer: No, page numbers are added as a separate layer and don't interfere with your existing content. The tool automatically adjusts spacing to ensure your content remains intact.

page_numbering.seo_content:
Page numbers are one of those details you only miss when they're absent — the moment a printed report scatters on the floor, or a reviewer asks you to "look at page 12" of a document that has no numbers. Adding page numbers to a PDF makes it easy to navigate, reference and reassemble, and gives any document a more finished, professional feel.

PDFOrca stamps clear, consistent page numbers onto every page of your PDF. Whether it's a contract, a thesis, a manual or a set of meeting papers, numbered pages make the document far easier to work with.

When numbering helps

  • Reports and proposals where readers need to cite specific pages.
  • Legal and academic documents that require numbered pages by convention.
  • Printed handouts that must stay in order if they're dropped or reshuffled.
  • Merged bundles where several files have become one long document.

Numbering that reads cleanly

Consistent placement is what makes numbering look professional — the same position on every page, clear of your text and margins. Numbering is especially valuable straight after combining documents, when a stack of separate files becomes a single sequence that needs a shared count. Because the numbers are added as a light overlay, the rest of your content stays exactly as it was.

Related tools

Page numbering is usually the finishing touch. Merge your files into one document first, tidy the sequence with Organize PDF, then add numbers here so the final count is correct. If the finished document is large, Compress PDF will make it easier to distribute.

pdf_to_excel.title: PDF to Excel
pdf_to_excel.description: Extract tables from your PDF files and convert them into editable Excel spreadsheets. Our converter preserves rows, columns, and table structure for accurate data extraction.
pdf_to_excel.seo_h1: Convert PDF to Excel Online for Free
pdf_to_excel.seo_h2: How to Convert PDF to Excel

seo_faq.question: What kind of PDFs work best?
seo_faq.answer: PDFs with clearly structured tables — visible rows, columns, and borders — yield the best results. These are typically PDFs exported directly from Excel, accounting software, or database reports. Scanned PDFs (images of pages) are also supported: the tool runs OCR first to recognise the text before extracting the table structure.

seo_faq.question: Will all tables be extracted?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. The tool automatically scans every page of your PDF, detects all table structures, and creates separate Excel sheets for each one. If a single page contains multiple tables, each is extracted into its own sheet so the data stays organised.

seo_faq.question: Will merged cells and formatting be preserved?
seo_faq.answer: Merged cells, borders, and basic formatting are preserved as closely as possible. Complex conditional formatting or in-cell charts may need manual cleanup in Excel.

seo_faq.question: What Excel format will I receive?
seo_faq.answer: You receive an .xlsx file, which is the modern Excel format supported by Microsoft Excel (2007 and later), Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc, Apple Numbers, and virtually every other spreadsheet application. You can open and edit it immediately.

seo_faq.question: How do I improve accuracy for complex tables?
seo_faq.answer: For the cleanest extraction, make sure your PDF contains selectable text (not a scanned image). If it is a scan, run it through our OCR tool first. Tables with clear borders and consistent column widths convert most accurately. After conversion, spot-check merged cells and totals — a quick review catches the occasional misalignment.

pdf_to_excel.seo_content:
Getting numbers out of a PDF and back into a spreadsheet by hand is tedious and error-prone. Bank statements, price lists, financial reports and data tables are often only available as PDFs, yet you need them in Excel to sort, total or chart the figures. PDFOrca's converter extracts the tables from your PDF and rebuilds them as editable spreadsheet cells.

Rather than dumping everything into one column, the tool detects rows and columns and maps them into a proper .xlsx grid, so your data lands in cells you can immediately calculate with.

Who needs this

  • Accountants and bookkeepers pulling figures from statements and reports.
  • Analysts who receive data as PDFs but need it in a workbook.
  • Anyone reconciling invoices, price lists or inventory tables.
  • Researchers extracting published tables for their own analysis.

Getting the cleanest extraction

Conversion works best when the PDF contains real, selectable text arranged in clear tables — the kind produced by exporting from Excel or an accounting system. If your PDF is a scan (a photo of a page), the numbers are images, not text, so run it through OCR first to make them recognisable. Densely merged cells or unusual layouts may need a quick tidy-up after conversion, but the heavy lifting of transcribing figures is done for you.

Practical tips for accurate extraction

  • Check if text is selectable — open your PDF and try highlighting a number. If you can select it, the PDF contains real text and will convert cleanly. If you can't, it's a scanned image and needs OCR first.
  • Extract only the pages you need — if your report is 50 pages but the table is on pages 8–10, use Extract Pages first. This produces a smaller, faster conversion with less cleanup.
  • Spot-check totals after conversion — verify that row and column totals match the original PDF. Merged cells or unusual formatting can occasionally shift a value into the wrong column.
  • Use filters and sorting immediately — once your data is in Excel, apply filters to quickly find outliers or missing entries. This is the main advantage of having the data in a spreadsheet rather than a static PDF.

Related tools

To go the other way and lock a finished workbook into a fixed document, use Excel to PDF. If you only need the table from one page of a long report, extract that page first with Extract Pages, then convert. Text-heavy PDFs that aren't really spreadsheets convert better with PDF to Word.

excel_to_pdf.title: Excel to PDF
excel_to_pdf.description: Transform your Excel spreadsheets into professional PDF documents. Our converter maintains table structure, formatting, and supports multiple sheets for perfect conversion every time.

excel_to_pdf.seo_h2: How to Convert Excel to PDF

seo_faq.question: Will all sheets be included in the PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Every sheet in your Excel workbook is converted and included as separate pages in the output PDF, in the same order they appear in the workbook. Hidden sheets are skipped, so only visible data ends up in the final document.

seo_faq.question: Will the formatting be preserved?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Cell styles, background colours, borders, fonts, number formatting (currency, percentages, dates), and conditional formatting are all preserved in the output PDF. The result looks the same as what you see in Excel's Print Preview.

seo_faq.question: Does each sheet become a separate page?
seo_faq.answer: Sheets are paginated according to their print area, so a multi-sheet workbook produces multiple pages. You can adjust print settings in Excel before converting to control the layout.

seo_faq.question: What Excel formats are supported?
seo_faq.answer: Both the older .xls format (Excel 97–2003) and the modern .xlsx format (Excel 2007 and later) are fully supported. Simply upload whichever version you have and the converter handles the rest.

seo_faq.question: What if my spreadsheet is wider than the page?
seo_faq.answer: If your table has many columns, set a print area and choose landscape orientation or 'Fit to Width' in Excel before uploading. This tells the converter how to lay out the columns so they don't spill onto extra pages. You can also reduce the font size slightly to fit more data per page.

Spreadsheets are living documents — full of formulas, hidden columns and layouts that shift depending on who opens them. When you need to send figures to someone for review, signing or the record, a PDF is the safer format: it freezes the numbers and layout exactly as you intended and can't be accidentally edited or recalculated by the recipient.

PDFOrca converts Excel workbooks (.xls and .xlsx) into clean PDFs that preserve your table structure, formatting, column widths and page breaks. What prints from Excel is what appears in the PDF — no surprise reflow, no broken cells.

When to convert a spreadsheet to PDF

  • Sending invoices, quotes and budgets that shouldn't be altered after they leave your hands.
  • Reports and dashboards shared with people who don't have Excel.
  • Official submissions where only PDF uploads are accepted.
  • Archiving a workbook so it stays readable regardless of software versions.

Keeping wide tables tidy

The most common issue converting spreadsheets is a table that's wider than the page, spilling columns onto extra sheets. Setting your print area and choosing landscape orientation or "fit to width" in Excel before converting produces a far cleaner PDF. Freeze panes and gridlines translate as you'd expect, and multi-sheet workbooks convert with each sheet flowing into the document.

Practical tips before you convert

  • Set a print area — in Excel, select the cells you want in the PDF and go to Page Layout → Print Area → Set Print Area. This prevents empty rows and columns from appearing as blank pages in the output.
  • Use Print Preview first — Excel's Print Preview (Ctrl + P) shows exactly how the page will break. Adjusting margins, orientation (portrait vs landscape), and scaling here saves a round trip.
  • Hide sheets you don't need — right-click any sheet tab and choose Hide. Hidden sheets are excluded from the conversion, so only the data you want to share ends up in the PDF.
  • Check number formatting — make sure currency symbols, decimal places, and date formats are correct before converting. The PDF captures the displayed value, not the underlying cell formula.

Related conversions

If you receive figures trapped in a PDF and need them back in cells, PDF to Excel reverses the process and recovers your data into a workbook. To bundle a converted spreadsheet with a cover letter or report, use Merge PDF, and shrink a chart-heavy result for email with Compress PDF.

ai_tools.seo_content:
Reading a 50-page report just to find two key takeaways wastes time most people don't have. PDFOrca's AI PDF tools let you upload any PDF and get a concise summary, a list of key points, and even a suggested title — all in seconds, right inside your browser.

What the AI actually does

When you upload a PDF, our system extracts the full text and passes it through a large language model. The model reads the entire document in context, identifies the most important ideas, and returns a structured analysis. You get three outputs:

  • Summary — a condensed version of the document that captures the main argument, findings, or narrative without filler.
  • Key points — a bullet list of the most important facts, figures, or conclusions, ranked by relevance.
  • Suggested title — a descriptive title generated from the content, useful when filenames like final_v3_revised.pdf tell you nothing.

Who benefits most

Anyone who works with long documents regularly will save hours every week:

  • Students and researchers processing dozens of papers can quickly decide which ones are worth a full read and which can be skimmed.
  • Legal professionals reviewing contracts, case files, or regulatory filings can surface the clauses and findings that matter most.
  • Business teams dealing with quarterly reports, pitch decks, and strategy documents can share AI-generated summaries instead of forwarding 40-page attachments.
  • Technical writers can feed manuals into the tool to check whether the document's key points match the intended message.

Privacy and security

Tips for the best results

Text-based PDFs produce the most accurate summaries. If your document is a scanned image, run it through our OCR tool first to convert images to selectable text. For very large documents (200+ pages), consider splitting the PDF into chapters and analysing each one separately — the model gives sharper results when the input is focused.

After analysis, you can download a polished DOCX report containing the summary, key points, and metadata. It's ready to attach to an email or drop into a shared drive without additional formatting.

PDFOrca's AI tools pair well with the rest of the suite. Merge several reports before analysis to get a combined summary, or compress the output report before sharing it.

seo_faq.question: Is AI PDF analysis free on PDFOrca?
seo_faq.answer: Yes, our AI PDF analysis tools are completely free. You can summarize, extract key points, and generate titles from any PDF without registration, payment, or hidden limits.

seo_faq.question: What types of PDFs work with the AI tools?
seo_faq.answer: Our AI works best with text-based PDFs such as reports, research papers, contracts, articles, and ebooks. Scanned PDFs (image-only) should be processed through our OCR tool first to convert them to selectable text before analysis.

seo_faq.question: How accurate is the AI summarization?
seo_faq.answer: The summarization uses a modern large language model that reads the full document context. Accuracy is high for well-structured documents with clear language. Results may vary for heavily technical jargon, tables-only content, or documents with unusual formatting.

seo_faq.question: Is my PDF data secure and private?
seo_faq.answer: Absolutely. Files are uploaded over an encrypted connection, processed in memory, and deleted automatically after analysis. We never store your documents on our servers. No account is required, and analysis results stay in your browser session only.

seo_faq.question: What languages does the AI support?
seo_faq.answer: Our AI model supports multiple languages including English, Hindi, Spanish, French, German, and many more. It can summarize and extract key points from documents written in any of these languages.

seo_faq.question: Can I download the AI analysis as a report?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. After analysis is complete, you can download a professionally formatted DOCX report containing the summary, key points, suggested title, and document metadata. The report is ready to share or archive.

about.title: About PDFOrca
about.description: A free, privacy-first toolkit for everyday PDF work, built from India for the world.
about.hero_heading_part2: that respect your time and privacy
about.hero_description: PDFOrca is an independent, privacy-first PDF toolkit created by Shahrukh from India. We built it because existing tools hide essential features behind paywalls, plaster pages with ads, or quietly ship your files to servers you know nothing about.
about.mission_subtitle: Make essential PDF work fast, free, and private
about.mission_text: Most PDF websites bury basic features behind subscriptions, riddle their pages with ads, or quietly send your files to servers you don't know. We don't. Every core tool here is free, every file is deleted within an hour, and we never sell user data.
about.values_subtitle: A short list of things we won't compromise on
about.cta_description: Pick any PDF tool and use it without signing up. Or reach out — we read every message.
about.mission_text_2: We focus on the tools people actually use day to day — merge, split, compress, convert — and we keep the interface uncluttered so you can finish what you came for in seconds.
about.value_privacy_desc: Your files are processed in temporary memory and auto-deleted within 1 hour. No backups. No tracking pixels. No selling data.
about.value_free_desc: Every essential PDF tool is free with no signup wall. Paid tiers (when launched) only unlock larger files and priority processing.
about.value_local_title: Built for India and the world
about.value_local_desc: Native Hindi interface, support for Indian document types (Aadhaar, government forms), and pricing in INR. We work hard for users underserved by US-first tools.
about.value_ai_desc: Our AI features run on Groq (cloud) with a local HuggingFace fallback. Document text is sent for analysis, never the file itself, and never used for training.
about.story_para_1: PDFOrca started when Shahrukh, an independent developer based in India, got tired of the same pattern on every PDF website: two free conversions, then a paywall — or worse, bloated output designed to push you toward a premium plan.
about.story_para_2: So he built his own set of tools. Then added a Hindi interface because millions of Indian users were underserved by English-only platforms. Then came AI-powered summarization after students kept asking for a faster way to digest research papers. Today PDFOrca offers 21 tools covering everything from merging and splitting to OCR and watermarking.
about.story_para_3: PDFOrca is a one-person operation. Shahrukh handles the code, the content, and every support email personally. There is no venture funding, no marketing team, and no investor dashboard — just a developer who believes PDF tools should be free, fast, and honest about how they handle your files.
about.story_para_4: The roadmap is simple: keep improving the tools people already use, add new ones when there is genuine demand, and never compromise on privacy. If you have a suggestion or spot a bug, reach out — feedback from real users drives every update.
about.creator_bio: Shahrukh is a self-taught developer based in India who builds web tools that solve real, everyday problems. He created PDFOrca to give people a free, no-nonsense alternative to the overpriced PDF software that dominates the market. He writes every line of code, every blog post, and answers every support email himself.
about.tech_text: PDFOrca runs on Next.js for the frontend and Python for file processing. Every upload is handled over an encrypted HTTPS connection, processed in temporary server memory, and automatically deleted within one hour. The AI features use Groq's inference API with a HuggingFace fallback — document text is sent for analysis, never the raw file, and nothing is used for model training. The platform supports both English and Hindi with full right-to-left text handling where needed.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How PDFOrca protects your files and personal data.
privacy.s1_p1: PDFOrca ("we", "our", "us") is a free online toolkit for PDF processing operated from India. This policy explains what data we collect, why we collect it, and how we protect it. We comply with India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act) and align with global standards including the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
privacy.s2_p1: When you use a tool (merge, split, compress, OCR, AI summary, etc.), your file is sent to our servers, processed in temporary memory or a temporary directory, and the result is returned to you. We never use your files for training models, advertising, or any purpose beyond fulfilling your request.
privacy.s2_p2: A background sweeper deletes processed files and any temporary artifacts within 1 hour. There is no "delete file" button because there is nothing left to delete after that window.
privacy.s2_p3: Uploaded files are never written to our database, never backed up, and never replicated to third parties.
privacy.s3_p1: An account is optional. If you create one, we store:
privacy.s3_li1: Email address (used for login and password reset)
privacy.s3_li2: Hashed password (bcrypt — we never see your real password)
privacy.s3_li4: Activity history (which tools you used and when, never the file contents)
privacy.s3_p2: Account data is stored in MongoDB Atlas (managed cloud database) with encryption at rest. You can delete your account at any time from your dashboard settings; deletion is permanent and removes all associated activity history.
privacy.s4_p1: Our AI summarization, key points, and title generation features send your document's extracted text to Groq Cloud (an LLM provider) for analysis. Groq does not retain prompts beyond the duration needed to compute a response. The document file itself is never sent to Groq — only the extracted plain text. If our cloud LLM is unavailable, we fall back to a local model running on our servers, which keeps the data entirely within our infrastructure.
privacy.s5_p1_pre: We use a small number of
privacy.s5_p1_post: for login session management and language preference. We use privacy-friendly analytics (no personal identifiers, no cross-site tracking) to understand which tools are popular and improve them. We use Google AdSense to display advertisements, which may use cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits. You can opt out of personalized ads at any time via Google's Ad Settings (https://adssettings.google.com). See our
privacy.s6_li4: — error monitoring (no file contents, only stack traces)
privacy.s6_li7: — advertising (may use cookies for ad personalization)
privacy.s6_p2: Each of these providers has their own privacy commitments. We choose providers that don't resell data and offer GDPR-compliant data processing agreements.
privacy.s7_p1: PDFOrca is not intended for users under 18 in jurisdictions where minors require parental consent. Under India's DPDP Act, processing children's personal data requires verifiable parental consent. If you believe a child has created an account, contact us and we will remove it.
privacy.s8_li1: Access the personal data we hold about you
privacy.s8_li3: Delete your account and associated history
privacy.s8_li4: Export your activity data in a machine-readable format
privacy.s8_li5: Withdraw consent for non-essential processing at any time
privacy.s8_p2_pre: To exercise any of these rights, email us at
privacy.s8_p2_post: . We respond within 30 days as required under the DPDP Act.
privacy.s9_p1: Our primary servers are located in Europe (Hetzner, Falkenstein/Germany). MongoDB Atlas data is hosted in a region of your choice during signup. By using the service, you consent to your data being processed in these locations, which provide adequate protections under GDPR and DPDP Act.
privacy.s10_p1: We use industry-standard security: TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit, bcrypt for passwords, JWT for session management with short expiry, rate limiting against abuse, and a least-privilege server architecture. Despite our best efforts, no online service is 100% secure; we will notify affected users within 72 hours of any confirmed breach.
privacy.s11_p1: If we change this policy materially, we will email registered users and display a banner on the site. Continued use after the change date constitutes acceptance.
privacy.lang_disclaimer: This policy is offered in English and Hindi. The English version is the canonical legal text; the Hindi translation is provided for convenience.

pdf_to_powerpoint.title: PDF to PowerPoint
pdf_to_powerpoint.description: Convert PDF documents into editable PowerPoint presentations. Each page becomes a slide.

seo_faq.question: Will my slides be editable after conversion?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Each PDF page becomes a PowerPoint slide, and text that was real text in the PDF becomes editable text you can retype or restyle. Image-based pages come across as images you can still move and resize.

seo_faq.question: Do animations and transitions come back?
seo_faq.answer: No. Animations, transitions and speaker notes only exist in the original PowerPoint file, so they can't be recovered from a PDF. The static content of every slide is rebuilt faithfully.

seo_faq.question: What file format do I get?
seo_faq.answer: You get a standard .pptx file that opens in Microsoft PowerPoint, Apple Keynote and Google Slides.

seo_faq.question: Does it work with scanned PDFs?
seo_faq.answer: It does, but a scanned deck is made of page images, so the resulting slides will contain pictures rather than editable text. Run the file through our OCR tool first if you need the text to be selectable.

seo_faq.question: Can I edit the slides after converting to PowerPoint?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. Text boxes, shapes, and images are converted to native PowerPoint objects, so you can click into any element and edit it directly in PowerPoint or Google Slides.

pdf_to_powerpoint.seo_content:
When a slide deck reaches you as a PDF, it looks fine but can't be edited — you can't tweak a bullet, swap an image or reuse a slide in your own presentation. Converting PDF to PowerPoint rebuilds each page as an editable slide (.pptx) so you can open it in PowerPoint, Keynote or Google Slides and carry on working.

PDFOrca turns every page of your PDF into a slide, preserving the visual layout so headings, text blocks and images land where they belong. It's the fastest way to recover an editable deck when the original file has gone missing.

When you'll want this

  • Updating an old presentation that only survives as an exported PDF.
  • Reusing slides from someone else's deck in your own talk.
  • Fixing a typo or figure without rebuilding the whole presentation.
  • Rebranding a deck with new colours, logos and fonts.

What converts well

PDFs that were exported from PowerPoint or Keynote convert most cleanly, because the layout maps naturally back onto slides. Text that was real text in the PDF becomes editable text on the slide; pages that are essentially images come across as images you can still move and resize. Very intricate animations and transitions can't be recovered — those live only in the original file — but the static content of every slide is faithfully rebuilt.

Related conversions

To turn a finished deck back into a shareable, fixed document, use PowerPoint to PDF. If you only need a few slides, pull those pages out first with Extract Pages. And to email a large, image-rich deck, shrink the PDF with Compress PDF before or after converting.

ocr_pdf.title: OCR PDF
ocr_pdf.description: Make a scanned PDF searchable by adding an invisible text layer.
ocr_pdf.language_hint: Pick the language that matches the text in your scan. Mixed-language docs work best with the primary language.

seo_faq.question: What does OCR actually do to my PDF?
seo_faq.answer: It reads the scanned image, recognises the printed characters, and adds an invisible layer of real text behind the page. The document looks identical but becomes searchable and its text can be selected and copied.

seo_faq.question: Can I edit the text after running OCR?
seo_faq.answer: OCR makes the text selectable and searchable inside the PDF. To fully edit it, convert the OCR'd file to Word with our PDF to Word tool, which turns the recognised text into an editable document.

seo_faq.question: Does OCR work on handwriting?
seo_faq.answer: OCR is designed for printed and typed text, which it recognises reliably. Handwriting is far less predictable and results vary a lot, so it isn't recommended for handwritten notes.

seo_faq.question: How can I improve accuracy?
seo_faq.answer: Use a clean, straight scan at around 300 DPI with good contrast. Straightening a skewed page and making sure it isn't upside down before running OCR both improve the result significantly.

seo_faq.question: What languages does the OCR engine support?
seo_faq.answer: The OCR engine supports English, Hindi, and most Latin-script languages. Best results come from clean scans at 200–300 DPI with good contrast.

ocr_pdf.seo_content:
A scanned document looks like text, but to a computer it's just a picture. You can't search it, select a phrase, or copy a paragraph out of it. Optical character recognition (OCR) reads the image, recognises the letters and numbers, and adds a hidden layer of real, selectable text behind the scan — turning a flat image into a searchable, copyable PDF.

PDFOrca's OCR tool processes scanned PDFs and image-based documents so you can finally search inside them, highlight passages, and copy content into other programs. The page still looks exactly the same; it just becomes intelligent underneath.

Why OCR matters

  • Searchability — find a name or figure instantly in a long scanned report.
  • Copy and reuse — lift text out of an old document instead of retyping it.
  • Accessibility — screen readers can read recognised text aloud.
  • Onward conversion — OCR is the step that makes PDF to Word and PDF to Excel work on scans.

Getting the most accurate results

OCR accuracy depends heavily on scan quality. A clean, straight scan at 300 DPI in good contrast recognises almost perfectly; a faint, skewed or low-resolution photo produces more mistakes. Straightening a crooked scan with Rotate PDF beforehand helps, as does making sure the page isn't upside down. Printed text is recognised far more reliably than handwriting.

Where OCR fits in your workflow

Run OCR first whenever you plan to convert a scan into an editable format — it's what lets the words come through as text rather than pictures. After OCR, you can compress the searchable PDF for sharing, or merge several recognised documents into one searchable archive.

seo_faq.question: How do I add my signature to the PDF?
seo_faq.answer: Upload the document, place your signature where it needs to go on the page, and download the signed file. You can position it precisely on the signature line before saving.

seo_faq.question: Is an electronic signature legally valid?
seo_faq.answer: In many everyday situations an electronic signature is widely accepted, but requirements vary by country and document type. For agreements with strict legal standards, confirm whether the recipient requires a certified, identity-verified digital signature.

seo_faq.question: Can I stop my signature from being moved afterwards?
seo_faq.answer: Yes. After signing, lock the document with a password using our Protect PDF tool so the file can't be easily altered by the recipient.

seo_faq.question: Do I need to print anything?
seo_faq.answer: No. The whole point is to avoid printing and scanning — you sign on screen and download a ready-to-send signed PDF.

seo_faq.question: Is the signature legally binding?
seo_faq.answer: PDFOrca adds a visual signature image to your PDF. For legal validity, check your jurisdiction's requirements — many accept electronic signatures, but some contexts require qualified digital certificates.

sign_pdf.seo_content:
Printing a document just to sign it, then scanning it back in, is a waste of time and paper. Signing a PDF electronically lets you add your signature to contracts, forms and agreements directly on screen and send them straight back — no printer, no scanner, no delay. PDFOrca lets you place your signature onto a PDF and download the signed file ready to return.

You can position your signature exactly where it belongs — on the signature line, next to your initials, or across a form field — and save it into the document. The result is a clean, signed PDF that looks the way a returned agreement should.

When it saves the day

  • Employment and rental contracts that need a quick signature and return.
  • Consent and permission forms for school, medical or travel purposes.
  • Business agreements and quotes you want to approve without printing.
  • Any form that says "sign and send back" when you're working remotely.

Making your signed document look right

Place your signature so it sits naturally on the line rather than floating over the text, and keep it at a sensible size. Once you've signed, it's often worth protecting the file so the signature can't easily be moved — you can lock the finished document with a password using Protect PDF. For agreements with strict legal requirements, check whether the recipient needs a certified digital signature, which is a distinct, identity-verified technology.

Related tools

If the form arrived as several files, merge them into one before signing. Need to sign only the last page of a long contract? Pull it out with Extract Pages first. And once signed, compress the PDF if it's too large to email back comfortably.
